text:
September 3rd: Part 2 (93②, Kugatsu Mikka (Ni)) is the 86 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

After Gon calls him to see if they've received any tips about the Phantom Troupe, Leorio tells him that it's only been red herrings so far, with one kid calling as a prank and claiming to be No. 6. He adds that he changed the ad to read "specify location" so that they can reduce the number of fake tips. He asks about their search for more hidden items, and Gon says that they found four with traces of aura.

In a flashback, Gon spots a vase with aura around it and sees that it has a bid of 500 from someone named Zepile. He remembers Killua's words about raising the price by 250% if there's a previous bid so that things aren't drawn out in a bidding war. However, Gon isn't sure what 250% of 500 is, guessing that it's 1,750. He returns to another stand and checks out a picture that he says still looks like a bunch of scribbles. Having crossed out the initial bid of 1,000, Gon sees that his 2,500 bid was crossed out by Zepile and replaced by a new 10,000 bid.

Killua calls Gon with his Beatle-07 and tells him that someone named Zepile quadrupled his bid on two items, realizing that he's using the same strategy to find rare items. Gon wonders what they should do, and Killua replies that they should put in a bid right before noon, hoping that they can win at least two items. Back in the present, Gon tells Leorio that they won three items while Zepile won the vase. As Gon and Killua head to register at an auction, a silhouette is seen watching them in the distance.

At the auction house, a man apologizes to Gon and Killua, telling them that their current catalog was finished six months ago and that they are considering items for next year. He explains that determining the authenticity of items takes time, adding that most of the other auction houses will tell them the same thing. Killua says that they want to sell their items by September 5, and the man suggests visiting an antiques dealer. Gon says that they prefer an auction, and while the man doesn't advise it, he says that they can show them at a dealers market, which is explained to be an auction for middlemen who only need a certificate to participate. It's added that bidding is limited to the items on display and that items can be added at the last minute, with bidders needing to use their judgment and appraisal skills to score the best deal.

The man explains that the prices will be lower than at public auctions, but that they can get a higher price compared to an antique shop. He warns them that the business is full of shady and untrustworthy people, telling them to make sure that their broker isn't lying or misinformed. The two thank him before leaving and realize that the dealers market is the only way to find out the value of the items they bought.

They enter a building and decide to have the broker just take a look at the items. The man first looks at the picture and asks them if they brought it from home, with Killua saying that they found the items in an old storehouse and pretending that he got his parents' permission to sell them if they could get a good price. The man explains that the picture is a signed lithograph by Mukatolini and that he only signed the ones he really liked. He authenticates the signature and says that it could sell for around 150,000, with Gon and Killua realizing that they can make a profit of 139,000.

The man then examines the handmade antique doll and praises its condition, revealing that it could sell for 300,000 (a profit of 249,500). The last item, however, the man says is worthless since the box is clearly newer than the statue itself. Killua guesses that their strategy wasn't foolproof, and Gon says that maybe the artist wasn't famous. The man explains that the carving on the statue is rough and that without even the sculptor's name as a hallmark, it's probably just the work of someone having fun a couple hundred years ago. He says that 1,500 would be a generous offer, and Gon and Killua feel bad about possibly losing 5,500.

However, the man then reveals that the wood itself is valuable since modern sculptors treasure it, saying that he knows someone who would pay 100,000 for it. He agrees to buy the wood for 80,000 if they sell the other two items for 420,000. Gon and Killua ask for a minute to think it over, and the man adds that no merchant would pay more than 450,000 for them, walking away to analyze the wood. Gon thinks that it may be better to sell them than pay the auction fees, and Killua adds that they could come back after getting a second opinion.

Another man suddenly enters and orders the broker to put the statue down, telling Gon and Killua that while he was telling truth about the first two items, he lied about the statue. He says that no antiques dealer would pay 80,000 for the wood and that what the man actually wants are the statue's contents. He explains that it's a wooden vault once used for tax evasion by wealthy people 300 years ago, adding that they are so well made that they are often sold by mistake. He guesses that the vault's seal is still intact, pointing to the seam and saying that only a professional can see it. He tells Gon and Killua that it should be full of riches and that the broker was probably planning to replace the contents while he pretended to analyze its age. The broker begins to stammer as the unknown man smiles and chuckles.
